alert_title: High-Confidence Malicious Activity from Tor Exit Node
alert_summary: A Tor exit node with a 100% confidence score and 92 total reports has been observed creating a user and attaching a user policy in the ap-southeast-1 region. This activity is highly suspicious and warrants immediate attention.
alert_reasoning: The presence of a high-confidence malicious source IP (185.220.101.47) with a confidence score of 100, categorized as a Bad Web Bot and Web App Attack, and identified as a Tor exit node, significantly increases the severity of this alert. The fact that this IP is creating a user and attaching a user policy in the ap-southeast-1 region suggests potential malicious intent. The historical correlation indicates that this is an isolated window, but the severity signal is still high due to the confidence score and categories of the malicious source IP. Severity upgraded from low to high per threat intelligence rules due to the high confidence score and malicious categories.
recommended_actions: Investigate the user creation and policy attachment activities in the ap-southeast-1 region
Review the IAM policies and users for any suspicious or unauthorized changes
Consider blocking the Tor exit node IP (185.220.101.47) to prevent further potential malicious activity
